Marie Okonkwo, 28 🇳🇬

Virtual Assistant Lagos, Nigeria English · Yoruba · French $14/hr ★ 87 composite

AI summary

Marie presents as a strong, well-rounded Virtual Assistant candidate with six years of executive support experience at finance and legal firms in Lagos. Communication scores are high across both interviews; references back her up. Two soft notes worth reading: slightly thinner advanced QuickBooks knowledge, and one reference still pending response. Recommend approval.

AI Interview 1 02

22 min · Apr 28, 9:14 AM WAT Passed
89/100 ★ Top tier
Communication
92
English fluency
88
Vocabulary range
85
Response coherence
90
Cultural fit
88
Strengths
  • Articulate, professional tone throughout
  • Clear specific examples with measurable outcomes
  • Active listening — asked clarifying questions twice
Watch
  • One filler-heavy pause around the 14-min mark (recovered well)
Q1 · 02:14

Tell me about a time you coordinated travel for an executive across multiple time zones.

"Last year my CEO needed to be in Singapore for a board meeting on Monday morning, then in London by Wednesday for an investor pitch. I built a buffer day in Dubai because long-haul flights with him meant he'd land sharp but jet-lagged — and the investor read on him mattered more than the schedule…"

Q4 · 11:38

How do you handle conflicting priorities from two senior stakeholders?

"I never assume one outranks the other in the moment. I get the deadlines from both, surface the conflict directly to the one with more flexibility, and propose two options. Most people just want to be asked, not surprised."

Transcript

AI commentary

Strong performance. Marie demonstrated composure, structure, and ownership in her answers. The travel coordination story showed real judgment about human factors, not just logistics. No red flags. Recommend pass.

AI Interview 2 03

35 min · Apr 28, 11:02 AM WAT Passed
84/100 High tier
Problem-solving
87
Domain knowledge
78
Time management
86
Tool proficiency
83
Professional judgment
84
Strengths
  • Excellent calendar & logistics reasoning under pressure
  • Strong communication frameworks (3-options pattern)
  • Deep familiarity with Notion, Slack, Gmail workflows
Concerns
  • Advanced QuickBooks features (reconciliation, A/R aging) — mid-level confidence
  • Less hands-on with Salesforce vs. HubSpot
Q7 · 18:42 — domain probe

Walk me through how you'd reconcile a month-end QuickBooks discrepancy with the bank statement.

"I'd start with the bank rec report and look at outstanding deposits and uncleared checks first… honestly, for the deeper accruals work I'd loop in the bookkeeper. I can flag the gap and structure the question, but I wouldn't claim to close it alone."

Transcript

AI commentary

Solid round. Where Marie hedged on QuickBooks she did so honestly — naming the limit rather than bluffing — which we score positively for self-awareness. Recommend match her with executive support / EA roles rather than full bookkeeping engagements.

Profile 04

Last edited: Apr 28, 8:41 AM Editable post-approval
M

Six years supporting C-suite executives at Lagos-based finance and legal firms. Specialises in complex calendar management, international travel coordination, and discreet board-level admin. Trilingual in English (native), Yoruba (native), and French (intermediate B2). Currently available 30 hrs/week, time zone WAT (UTC+1).

"Anand has been one of the best hires I've made through any platform. He runs my eng leadership calendar tighter than I do, surfaces things I didn't know I needed surfaced, and never once dropped a ball."

— Sarah Lin, CEO Acme Co · Apr 12, 2026 · ★★★★★

Disputes 5 cases
1 open · 1 under review · 3 resolved
Disputes / DSP-2026-04-12

Sofia Reyes vs. Quill & Co OPEN

Filed: 3 days ago · Apr 27 Type: Payment delay Amount: $2,840 Days open: 3
S
CLAIMANT · TALENT
Sofia Reyes 🇲🇽
VETTED · 16 MONTHS · 4.3 RATING
vs.
QC
RESPONDENT · CLIENT
Quill & Co
EDITORIAL · 12 PPL · NEW YORK
Both parties have submitted statements · awaiting Atlas decision
Recommended action: review evidence and apply Net-30 contract terms. SLA target: respond within 7 days of filing (4 days remaining).
SLA · 4D LEFT
01

Claim summary

FROM CLAIMANT — SOFIA REYES

"The first invoice ($1,420) was paid 32 days late. The second invoice ($1,420) is now 18 days overdue. I have email threads, signed agreement showing Net-30 terms, and bank statement screenshots."

FROM RESPONDENT — ELEANOR VOSS, QUILL & CO

"I dispute the characterization. We had a documented agreement that invoices would be paid net-30, and the second was within that window. Will respond formally with our records this week."

02

Atlas summary

Both parties agree Net-30 was the contract term. The dispute hinges on which date triggers the clock — invoice issuance vs. work completion. Atlas standard interpretation: invoice issuance date starts Net-30. By that standard, Sofia's first invoice (issued Mar 8, paid Apr 9) ran 32 days. The second invoice (issued Apr 12, due May 12) is currently within window.

AI recommendation: Partial in favor of claimant. First invoice acknowledgment + late fee. Second invoice not yet due — dispute premature.
—

Case facts

Total amount in dispute
$2,840.00
Hours worked (unpaid)
200h · over 8 weeks
Contract terms
Net-30 · standard Atlas template
Engagement status
Paused pending dispute
Atlas backstop active
Up to 14 days · expires May 11
Both parties' track record
Sofia: 0 prior disputes · Quill: 0 prior

Score trend
14-month rolling
↑ 18 pts since start
Mar 2025 Sept 2025 Apr 2026 · 88
Steady upward trajectory. Crossed benchmark at month 6, top quartile at month 10. Notable lift in Q1 2026 from improved review accuracy and pool growth.
Strengths
3 standout
Decision accuracy
94.2% of decisions confirmed by admin review · top 8% across all categories. Particularly strong on tier-upgrade calls.
Pool quality stewardship
Your pool's avg client rating climbed from 4.4 → 4.7 over 14 months · biggest delta in your category. Strategic re-cert decisions clearly working.
Talent relationship depth
Avg 4.8/5 from your candidates on responsiveness and care · 3 unsolicited testimonials in the last quarter.
Growth areas
2 to focus on
Sourcing pipeline conversion
28% sourced→applied · benchmark is 34%. Stalled prospects sit longer in Contacted than peer median (avg 5.4d vs 4.1d). Consider tightening outreach cadence.
Client expansion conversion
Of your 12 clients, only 2 expanded engagements in the last quarter · benchmark is 4. Look for cross-sell opportunities at quarterly check-ins.
Suggested skill development
Your dispute-handling style is strong but limited to 3 cases. Consider Atlas's Advanced mediation training — fits your trajectory toward Senior Specialist.

Operational workflow

Reviews & approvals

Sort: Oldest first
REV-2026-04-29-007 · INCOMING

Off-board recommendation: Mei Chen

Submitted by: System auto-trigger Type: Off-board decision Filed: 2 days ago · Apr 28 SLA: 3 days remaining
Atlas system recommendation
Escalate · requires specialist judgment
Mei Chen has crossed the auto-pause threshold (3 consecutive engagements ≤ 4.0★) but has filed an appeal citing scope-creep that wasn't escalated mid-engagement. Standard policy is auto-offboard after 30 days paused without remediation. Mei's appeal puts the case in a judgment zone — Atlas system flags this for specialist decision rather than auto-action.
Subject context
Pulled from candidate record
Talent
Mei Chen 🇨🇳
Tier · tenure

4.0 / 5

1,240h
Disputes
0 (clean)
Status
Paused · review
Discussion thread · 4 messages
Audit-logged
Atlas System AUTO-TRIGGERED
2 DAYS AGO · 9:14 AM
Auto-pause triggered for Mei Chen — 3 consecutive engagements rated below 4.3★. Appeal filed on file. Routing to specialist for review per policy POL-23-OFFBOARD-EX.
JT
Jamie Torres ADMIN · POOL OPS
2 DAYS AGO · 11:42 AM
Miguel — wanted your judgment on this one. Mei's record is clean (0 disputes, 14mo tenure, 1,240h logged). The 3-low-rating threshold triggers auto-offboard, but her appeal cites scope-creep with the M&A finance engagement that was outside her stated profile. Worth a deeper look before we cut her loose.
MR
Miguel Ramos YOU · SPECIALIST
YESTERDAY · 2:08 PM
I've reviewed Mei's three flagged engagements. Two were SaaS ops (her core), one was M&A finance research (outside her profile, accepted under client pressure). The M&A engagement is where two of the three sub-4.3 ratings came from. Recommendation: do not off-board. Pause for action — 60 days, with a written commitment to refuse engagements outside her profile. If she takes another sub-4.3 in her core area, then off-board.
JT
Jamie Torres ADMIN · POOL OPS
TODAY · 10:18 AM
That reasoning lands. I'd lean approve on your recommendation — but flagging that this would be a precedent (we'd be reading "scope-mismatch" as a mitigating factor in the auto-offboard rule). Want to make sure that's intentional. Confirm and I'll co-sign.
↵ to send · ⇧↵ for new line · audit-logged
